file_insights.purpose: Celery application configuration and periodic task scheduling
Register Celery app for Django auto-discovery
changes: Ensure Celery app is loaded when Django starts

patterns_discovered.description: Comprehensive .env.example with detailed comments, grouped sections, and production warnings
evidence: Security note: 'CRITICAL: Generate a unique secret key for production'
Clear comments for each configuration variable

patterns_discovered.description: Task routing strategy using dedicated queues by domain (AI, analytics, notifications, etc.)
evidence: Task route patterns matching app modules to specific queues

patterns_discovered.description: Well-organized beat schedule with clear purpose and scheduling information
evidence: 6 periodic tasks defined with specific schedules
Cron expressions for precise timing control

patterns_discovered.description: Robust error handling and retry strategy for async tasks
evidence: task_acks_late = True for reliable message acknowledgment
Rate limiting annotations per task type

patterns_discovered.description: .env.example supports development, staging, and production configurations

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Django settings module hardcoded in celery.py
gotchas_discovered.impact: Could cause issues if Celery is started without proper environment variable override
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Ensure DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE is set correctly in production deployment

gotchas_discovered.description: Tasks are autodiscovered when Celery app is created, which happens when config/__init__.py is imported
gotchas_discovered.impact: All task modules must exist and be properly imported for autodiscovery to work without errors
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Task modules should be created before deploying to avoid ImportError

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Beat schedule task references without validation
gotchas_discovered.description: Periodic tasks reference task paths as strings (e.g., 'apps.analytics.tasks.aggregate_hourly_analytics') without validation
gotchas_discovered.impact: Missing or misspelled task paths won't be caught until runtime
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document task creation as prerequisite and test beat schedule startup

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Redis single instance for multiple purposes
gotchas_discovered.description: .env.example uses same Redis instance for caching (db=0) and Celery (db=1), with default concurrency of 4
gotchas_discovered.impact: Potential resource contention and insufficient worker capacity in high-load scenarios
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Production deployments should consider separate Redis instances and tune CELERY_WORKER_CONCURRENCY

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Email configuration in .env but no validation in settings
gotchas_discovered.description: EMAIL_HOST_USER and EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD can be empty strings, leading to silent SMTP failures
gotchas_discovered.impact: Email sending could fail silently in production if these aren't populated
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add validation in Django settings to raise errors for missing required email credentials

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created comprehensive Celery configuration and environment template
deliverables: Production-ready .env.example with 139 lines covering all major application concerns
Celery configuration with 6 periodic tasks, 7 dedicated queues, and resilience features
Django app initialization updated to auto-import Celery for shared_task support
Complete documentation in module docstrings and inline comments
approach_outcome.completeness: All required components implemented without requiring follow-up work
approach_outcome.code_quality: Well-structured, documented, and following Django/Celery best practices

recommendations.recommendation: Add environment-specific validation in Django settings
recommendations.rationale: Validate critical environment variables at startup to catch configuration errors early
recommendations.example: Raise ImproperlyConfigured if DJANGO_SECRET_KEY contains 'change-this' in production

recommendations.recommendation: Create management command for testing Celery configuration
recommendations.rationale: Provide easy way to verify Celery, Redis, and task discovery are working before production deployment
recommendations.example: python manage.py celery_test to run autodiscover_tasks and execute debug_task

recommendations.recommendation: Document all required task modules that Celery will autodiscover
recommendations.rationale: Prevent runtime ImportError when task files don't exist
recommendations.example: Create CELERY_SETUP.md listing expected task modules in each app

recommendations.recommendation: Add separate Redis instances for caching vs Celery in production
recommendations.rationale: Prevent resource contention between cache and message broker
recommendations.suggestion: Use REDIS_CACHE_URL and REDIS_BROKER_URL as separate environment variables

recommendations.recommendation: Implement Celery task monitoring and alerting
recommendations.rationale: Track failed tasks, worker health, and queue backlogs in production
recommendations.tools: Consider Flower (Celery monitoring tool) or similar observability solution

recommendations.recommendation: Add AWS credentials validation and S3 configuration verification
recommendations.rationale: Prevent media upload failures due to invalid AWS credentials
recommendations.suggestion: Add settings validation to verify AWS credentials work before accepting requests
